name: Keaton Mitchell
headline: Big-play handcuff in a crowded Chargers backfield
body:
Mitchell's calling card is detonating, not grinding. Across 13 games in 2025 he turned just 59 carries into 341 yards at 5.78 per tote, with a 16.95% explosive-run rate and four runs of 20-plus, including a 55-yarder. His rookie 2023 tape was even louder: 8.43 yards a carry, 25.5% explosive runs, and a 0.345 EPA per play on 47 attempts. The problem is volume and efficiency under pressure, only 2.17 yards after contact in 2025 and a slightly negative -0.041 EPA mark, signaling a player who feasts in space but rarely creates it himself.

Now in Los Angeles, he sits behind Omarion Hampton and battles Kimani Vidal for the change-of-pace role. Projections peg him near 85 carries, 395 yards, and roughly 59 fantasy points, modest standalone value that spikes only if Hampton misses time. Treat him as a deep-league speculative stash and Hampton's lottery-ticket handcuff.
